game_name: ToeJam & Earl in Panic on Funkotron

danger_signals.visual_form: Antagonistic human characters visible on screen or detected by radar
danger_signals.meaning: Active threat requiring capture/combat; signals invasion state of Funkotron

danger_signals.visual_form: Radar display showing Earthling distance and location
danger_signals.meaning: Danger in vicinity; enemy hidden in environment or nearby; threat level increases as proximity closes

danger_signals.signal: Environmental hazards in Hyper Funk Zone
danger_signals.visual_form: Dynamic obstacles appearing in specialized gateway areas
danger_signals.meaning: High-risk zones requiring evasion; danger state escalation

safety_signals.visual_form: Collectable gift boxes scattered throughout levels
safety_signals.meaning: Resource availability; safer progression path with super jars, radar scans, teleporting moves

safety_signals.visual_form: Interactive environmental objects marked with coin/meter visual
safety_signals.meaning: Safe interaction points; trigger beneficial events or mini-games

safety_signals.visual_form: Ten hidden objects belonging to Lamont the Funkapotamus
safety_signals.meaning: Collectible safety markers; presence indicates positive world-state progression toward best ending

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Funky alien aesthetic; distinct character models
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Funkotron planet environment; funk-themed architecture and aesthetics

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Human character models contrasting with alien environment
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Multiple antagonistic Earthling types visible in gameplay
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Invading human presence spreading across Funkotron; causes panic and displacement of native creatures

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Lamont artifacts serve as visual representation; source of all funk
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Lamont's exile to another dimension signals world-state crisis; his scattered artifacts mark restoration path

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Earthlings present throughout environment; panic visible in NPC behavior; Lamont absent from world
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Earthlings behave antagonistically; ToeJam and Earl must actively hunt and capture; radar system necessary to locate hidden threats

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Fewer Earthlings visible; captured specimens removed from environment; progress toward clearing areas
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Players advance through levels; can move to next zone once all Earthlings captured

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Gateway transitions to specialized high-risk visual aesthetic with dynamic obstacles
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player must navigate obstacles rather than hunt; alternative progression path; higher reward potential (presents)

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: All ten Lamont artifacts collected and displayed; world gradually returns to stable funk state
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Culminates in best ending; indicates successful restoration of Funkotron balance

environmental_storytelling.example: Radar mechanic communicates that Earthlings are hidden throughout environment; world-state of infiltration and concealment
Ten hidden Lamont objects distributed across levels; visual markers of the Funkapotamus's displacement; collecting them restores world balance
Human invasion physically spreads across Funkotron's visual landscape; panic spreading among native population communicated through character presence and behavior
Power-up placement signals safe resource zones and progression pathways; visual economy of funk restoration
Coin-operated environmental objects indicate civilization/infrastructure points and safe interaction zones within invaded world

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Visible on screen or hidden in environment; detected by radar; pose direct threat requiring jar capture
Removed from environment; collected in jars; no longer present as threat
Use radar to locate Earthlings; leap over obstacles; attack with jars; collect presents and artifacts
Not visible in world; only represented through scattered artifacts; presence communicated through narrative and artifact mechanic
Affected by Earthling invasion; visual manifestation of panic state through character presence and displacement

notes: This game communicates world-state primarily through presence/absence of threat entities and collectible restoration objects. The radar mechanic is central to danger signaling—it makes hidden threats visible to player, reflecting the invasion/infiltration world-state. Faction identity is communicated through visual contrast: alien funk aesthetic vs. human invaders. The Lamont artifact collection system directly ties world-state restoration to visual markers scattered across levels. Danger increases as Earthlings concentrate in areas; safety increases through present collection and artifact accumulation. The shift to side-scrolling platformer obscures some political nuance from the original game's design, but the core narrative of planetary defense and restoration remains visually encoded.
